gaps.summary: Optum patient IM1 supported-test pairing remains outside lawful local browser automation because the real supplier tenant and pairing pack are controlled externally.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Apply the saved manifest row manually in the supplier portal, capture masked evidence, then confirm the binding hash and authoritative-read policy against the generated registry.
gaps.followUpAction: Replace this bridge only after supplier-approved non-production credentials and portal-safe automation consent exist.

gaps.summary: TPP patient IM1 pairing uses supplier-controlled supported-test access and GP-issued linkage prerequisites that cannot be safely exercised from the repo harness.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Keep the row manifest-backed, prove policy and binding identity locally, and record only masked evidence from the real portal when access is granted.
gaps.followUpAction: Promote to automated only if a lawful supported-test browser tenant is issued for repeated CI-safe exercise.

gaps.summary: The transaction/API local-component pairing still depends on provider-side portal and practice-host install steps that this workstation must not mutate directly.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Use the local portal twin for automated callback proof, then require a human checkpoint for the actual provider-side pairing and local-component install pack.
gaps.followUpAction: Automate only the twin until the provider explicitly approves unattended supported-test mutation.

gaps.summary: GP Connect onboarding is a direct-care consumer process with HSCN, JWT, and mTLS prerequisites that cannot be fully emulated as a supplier portal mutation from this repo.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Keep the manifest row explicit, verify direct-response and later-read posture locally, and treat real onboarding as an operator-controlled bridge with masked artifacts only.
gaps.followUpAction: Revisit when a sanctioned integration tenant and automated evidence path are available.
